name: RAG AI Agent with Milvus and Cohere

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## Why Milvus
Based on comparisons and user feedback, **Milvus is often considered a more performant and scalable vector database solution compared to Supabase**, particularly for demanding use cases involving large datasets, high-volume vector search operations, and multilingual support.


### Requirements
- Create an account on [Zilliz]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} to generate the Milvus cluster. 
- There is no need to create docker containers or your own instance, Zilliz provides the cloud infraestructure to build it easily
- Get your credentials ready from Drive, Milvus (Zilliz), and [Cohere]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

### Usage
Every time a new pdf is added into the Drive folder, it will be inserted into the Milvus Vector Store, allowing for the interaction with the RAG agent in seconds.

## Calculate your company's RAG costs

Want to run Milvus on your own server on n8n? Zilliz provides a great [cost calculator]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

### Get in touch with us
Want to implement a RAG AI agent for your company? [Shoot us a message]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

parameters.content:
## Why Milvus
Based on comparisons and user feedback, **Milvus is often considered a more performant and scalable vector database solution compared to Supabase**, particularly for demanding use cases involving large datasets, high-volume vector search operations, and multilingual support.

- There is no need to create docker containers or your own instance, Zilliz provides the cloud infraestructure to build it easily
- Get your credentials ready from Drive, Milvus (Zilliz), and [Cohere]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

### Usage
Every time a new pdf is added into the Drive folder, it will be inserted into the Milvus Vector Store, allowing for the interaction with the RAG agent in seconds.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Drive  O Auth2  A P I details
open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Watch New Files".
Open the "Default Data Loader" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Embeddings Cohere" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "When chat message received" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Download New" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Insert into Milvus" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "RAG Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Retrieve from Milvus" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI 4o" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Memory" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Cohere embeddings" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Set Chunks" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Extract from File" node (n8n-nodes-base.extractFromFile) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
